title: No native esigning feature in Monday?
excerpt: Hi all, are there no native options in Monday.com for electronic signatures (ie in forms)?  I know Monday integrates with Docusign and a few others but hoping there might be a native solution that doesn’t require an inte…

posts.author: botsquad (Patrick Fallon)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi all, are there no native options in Monday.com for electronic signatures (ie in forms)? I know Monday integrates with Docusign and a few others but hoping there might be a native solution that doesn’t require an integration and yet another paid plan.

posts.author: Carson (Carson Butcher)
posts.cookedHtml:
@botsquad There is a signature feature in forms!

See here:

image951×593 56.4 KB

image948×592 42.9 KB

posts.author: colleen928 (Colleen Bonniol)
posts.cookedHtml: This is brilliant, so happy to stumble upon this thread. Do you know if Monday forms are considered legal documents? Would the signtures hold up in court? Thank you!

posts.author: botsquad (Patrick Fallon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Carson, I have tested and think I know the answer to this but in case I’m completely wrong…

It seems that it’s not possible to add text formatting of any kind (other than the Form description and any question descriptions) in Monday. Is this correct? I have a client wanting to use Forms for Onboarding forms that are like contracts with quite a lot of text, bullet points etc and it seems Forms won’t do this…
Unless I am mistaken?

posts.author: botsquad (Patrick Fallon)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Colleen, I am still learning about Forms in Monday (obviously) but I have done quite a lot of research into digital contracts in the past. The general legal consensus appears to be that the harder it is for the wrong person to sign an electronic contract, the more binding it is legally. Eg you could use Monday to create a contract but if anybody with access to the url can sign the contract, it becomes very difficult to prove that the signer is in fact who you think it is. If you want a secure and affordable option, check out https://esignatures.io, it allows you to require authentication from the signer on both mobile and email (if you want), you pay per contract and their contracts are super easy to edit. Hope this helps!

posts.author: Carson (Carson Butcher)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Patrick,

You’re spot on. Obviously, you could use dashes, emojis or other characters to embody some formatting, but there is no additional text formatting options, aside from customizing text direction, font, and text color on forms.

To give you an idea:

image783×676 34.4 KB

Spot-nik has an app called SuperForm - which has more functionality than standard work forms. This isn’t currently a feature, but something they may consider adding if there was enough interest. @guy.gavish, what do you think about a text formatting feature in form question descriptions?

posts.author: Carson (Carson Butcher)
posts.cookedHtml:
Adding to what Patrick said, the binding of a signature is almost entirely dependent on being able to prove who signed it, and proof nothing was tampered with.

Unfortunately, to my knowledge there’s no way to verify either of these with 100% certainty using workforms.

The main issue is that items created through forms don’t indicate who the creator (submitter) is, even if you’re a member of the account. So, realistically anyone could “submit & sign” even using the advanced features.

See below:

image1218×365 19.2 KB

With this being said, there are apps on the marketplace which allow you to create legally binding signed documents with. GetSign come to mind, by Jetpack Apps. @Nir-Jetpack could tell you more.

Hope this helps!

posts.author: Nir-Jetpack (Nir @ Jetpack Team)
posts.cookedHtml: @patrickfallon Did you try GetSign - It a fully integrated, Legally binding solution for Generating Documents, Capturing customer data and eSignature solution. Let me know your thoughts!

posts.author: Vecino (Jonathan Neighbour)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi - I have been experimenting with WorkForms within an existing board we use to allocate jobs to our field based teams.

These jobs require a site manager sign off for invoicing, which we are currently fulfilling manually with a hard copy signed form which is attached to our invoice.

I have been able to capture a signature using WorkForms, but only using a form which creates a new item.

We need the opposite, whereby a signature is captured onto an existing item.

I have been advised that JotForm and SuperForm are third party options, however they offer considerably more functionality than we require and are quite expensive for our use case.

Is there a native option or can we use Adobe of any of the Microsoft Office suite which we already subscribe to? We only need an electronic squiggle to attach, it doesn’t need to be legally binding.

posts.author: samicaracand (Sami Caracand (DocuGen))
posts.cookedHtml:
I have good news! We’re releasing e-signatures as a new feature in DocuGen starting next week. With DocuGen Sign you get the assurance of legally-admissible signatures thanks to a tamper-proof digital seal (like DocuSign):

  • Populate documents from board data
  • Send documents to multiple signers
  • Automate document sending using automations

We’re granting access to DocuGen Sign early adopters as soon as next week (10 June.) If you would like access, please fill out this form:

posts.author: JenefaJ (Jenefa)
posts.cookedHtml:
At the moment, monday.com itself doesn’t have a truly native eSignature tool inside forms.

That’s exactly why we built The PDF Maker for monday. It adds official, legally binding eSignatures directly into your monday workflows — no extra tools needed:

  • Signature fields in forms → Add an eSignature field to any form created with The PDF Maker inside monday. Customers or colleagues can sign directly in the form.

  • Verification & compliance → Signers can be verified with OTP codes, and every signature includes audit trails, timestamps, and encrypted storage to meet legal standards.

  • Document workflows → Draft documents from board data, send them for signing, and automatically attach the signed, read-only PDF back to the board item.

  • All-in-one → No need for DocuSign or third-party subscriptions — you can collect signatures, track statuses, and store signed files entirely within monday.

Full guide: How to collect eSignatures on your documents using monday.com

posts.author: krasten (Kristen Harris)
posts.cookedHtml: This is exactly what I’m looking for as well!
